groove_from.desc: Reunionese maloya as the DOMINANT frame: hypnotic 6/8 maloya pulse driven by kayamb, roulèr, sati and bobre; Creole call-and-response, ancestral Indian-Ocean trance

harmony_from.desc: acid blues only as a guest layer over the maloya, never the frame: minor pentatonic bends and a TB-303 acid bassline weaving through, subordinate to the percussion

production: raw, percussion-forward, tropical and live; the Reunionese percussion always in front, lo-fi but warm
tension: the 303 squelch threading through acoustic maloya percussion; electronic acid as a guest in a Reunionese trance, not a takeover

constraints.claim: Reunionese maloya percussion must lead; acid blues stays a guest layer
constraints.note: after v1 drifted to Soviet rock (cf. catalogue MR-001)

avoid: 70s blues-rock band, Eastern-European / Soviet rock, dominant electric guitar, anything erasing the maloya percussion; clean EDM gloss, festival drop
vocal: Creole call-and-response, raw, incantatory, in Reunion Creole

ressenti.text: v1 drifted to the Eastern bloc ('test-card broadcast, USSR-era Hungary'), the maloya vanished -> atom strengthened; see catalogue MR-001

groove_from.desc: deep one-drop bass, spacious off-beat skank, spring reverb and tape-echo throws

harmony_from.desc: minor-key saudade melody, Portuguese guitarra twelve-string ornaments, rubato melancholy

production: cavernous, echo-drenched, analog, lots of negative space
tension: the intimacy of a fado vocal against the vast empty space of dub; longing pulled through echo

constraints.note: a held position, not an objective truth: fado is defined by its language, per the maker

avoid: busy arrangement, bright EDM, fast tempo, non-Portuguese vocals (per the position above)

groove_from.desc: 160 bpm but stripped and spacious, kick-driven: syncopated kicks slightly off the grid (triplet swing) giving a rolling, hypnotic, tribal groove; sparse claps, one repeated vocal sample, lots of air

harmony_from.desc: monophonic modal Latin plainchant, no harmony, free-floating sacred melody

production: minimal, dry, kick-driven groove with lots of space, against wet cathedral reverb on the chant
tempo_feel: 160 bpm felt as a spacious hypnotic pulse, kicks doing the work, the chant floating freely over the displaced groove
tension: timeless monophonic chant riding a stripped, off-grid footwork kick; eternity over a hypnotic tribal pulse
avoid: busy hi-hat rolls, chopped-vocal chaos, density, juke/EDM cliché — footwork is stripped and kick-driven (corr. practitioner)
